ucs.title: [LLM] Machete (APT-C-43) Pyark/py2exe Python backdoor execution from user-writable paths
ucs.description: Hunts Machete's signature py2exe-compiled Python implants (Machete/Pyark/Numpy) which leave behind interpreter artifacts (python27.dll, library.zip, _ctypes.pyd) executing from %APPDATA%, %TEMP%, or %PROGRAMDATA% — frequently chained with a scheduled task for persistence. This is highly specific to Machete because almost no other tracked actor consistently ships py2exe Python loaders against LATAM military/government targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("python.exe","pythonw.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*python27.dll*","*library.zip*","*_ctypes.pyd*","*MSVCR90.dll*")) AND (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=schtasks.exe AND (Processes.process="*python*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as schtasks_cmd] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql: let py_exec = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where (FileName in~ ("python.exe","pythonw.exe")) or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("python27.dll","library.zip","_ctypes.pyd") | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\") | project PyTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, PyProc=FileName, PyPath=FolderPath, PyCmd=ProcessCommandLine, AccountName; let sched = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("python","AppData","Temp","ProgramData") and ProcessCommandLine has "/create" | project SchedTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, SchedCmd=ProcessCommandLine; py_exec | join kind=inner sched on DeviceId | where abs(datetime_diff('minute', PyTime, SchedTime)) <= 60 | project PyTime, SchedTime, DeviceName, AccountName, PyPath, PyCmd, SchedCmd
ucs.rationale: Machete is one of the very few espionage actors whose primary RAT (Pyark/Machete) is a py2exe-bundled Python program — leaving telltale interpreter artifacts on disk. Combining the artifact path with a near-time schtasks /create on the same host narrows to Machete's documented install→persistence pattern targeting Venezuelan/Ecuadorian/Colombian military and government endpoints, sharply different

ucs.title: [LLM] Machete Spanish-language military-themed spearphish → RAR/ZIP self-extractor → MSIexec install chain
ucs.description: Hunts Machete's characteristic delivery chain: Spanish-language lures impersonating LATAM military/intelligence bodies (Comando, Ejército, Fuerza Armada, FANB, comunicado, informe) carrying RAR/ZIP self-extractors, followed within minutes by MSIexec or a freshly-dropped binary from %TEMP%/%APPDATA%. This pivots on Machete's unique combination of Spanish military lure language and T1218.007 install behavior on LATAM tenants.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime from datamodel=Email where (All_Email.subject IN ("*ejercito*","*ejército*","*fuerza armada*","*FANB*","*comando*","*comunicado*","*informe*","*orden de operaciones*","*inteligencia militar*","*ministerio de defensa*")) AND (All_Email.file_name IN ("*.rar","*.zip","*.lnk","*.7z","*.iso")) by All_Email.src_user All_Email.recipient All_Email.subject All_Email.file_name All_Email.message_id | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | rename recipient as user | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as proc_chain min(_time) as proc_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=msiexec.exe AND (Processes.process="*http*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*")) OR (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe") AND Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*")) by Processes.user Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where proc_time >= firstTime AND proc_time <= firstTime+3600 | table firstTime proc_time user dest src_user subject file_name process_name proc_chain
ucs.kql: let lure = EmailEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where Subject matches regex @"(?i)(ejercito|ej[eé]rcito|fuerza armada|FANB|comando|comunicado|informe|orden de operaciones|inteligencia militar|ministerio de defensa)" | join kind=inner (EmailAttachmentInfo | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(rar|zip|7z|iso|lnk|img)$") on NetworkMessageId | project MailTime=Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, FileName, SHA256; let sus_install = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where (FileName =~ "msiexec.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://",@"\AppData\",@"\Temp\"))) or (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe") and FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\",@"\Temp\") and FileName endswith ".exe") | project ProcTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn, ProcCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ProcFile=FileName, ProcParent=InitiatingProcessFileName; lure | join kind=inner sus_install on $left.RecipientEmailAddress == $right.AccountUpn | where datetime_diff('minute', ProcTime, MailTime) between (0 .. 60) | project MailTime, ProcTime, DeviceName, AccountUpn, SenderFromAddress, Subject, FileName, ProcParent, ProcFile, ProcCmd
ucs.rationale: Machete (aka APT-C-43, El Machete) is uniquely Spanish-speaking and has consistently used military/intelligence lure language in Spanish targeting Venezuelan FANB and other LATAM defense ministries — per ESET, Cylance and Securelist reporting. Most other state actors targeting these victims (e.g. Blind Eagle/APT-C-36) prefer banking or judicial lures, so the combination of Spanish military lexicon
